items.title: CVE-2026-41664: Integer overflow in memory copy size calculation in Samsung Open Source ONE coul...
items.description:
Integer overflow in memory copy size calculation in Samsung Open Source ONE could lead to invalid memory operations with large tensor shapes.
Affected version is prior to commit  1.30.0.
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2026-41664 vulnerability is an integer overflow in the memory copy size calculation within Samsung Open Source ONE, which could lead to invalid memory operations when dealing with large tensor shapes. This issue affects versions prior to commit 1.30.0, making them susceptible to exploitation. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to a denial-of-service or even code execution, affecting users of the Samsung Open Source ONE software. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their Samsung Open Source ONE software to version 1.30.0 or later, ensuring that the commit including the fix for CVE-2026-41664 is applied. Additionally, monitoring for unusual memory operations and implementing exploit mitigation techniques can help detect and prevent potential attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-40450: Integer overflow in output tensor copy size calculation in Samsung Open Source O...
items.description:
Integer overflow in output tensor copy size calculation in Samsung Open Source ONE could cause incorrect copy length and memory corruption for oversized tensors.
Affected version is prior to commit  1.30.0.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40450 is an integer overflow vulnerability in the output tensor copy size calculation in Samsung Open Source ONE, affecting versions prior to 1.30.0. This vulnerability can cause incorrect copy length and memory corruption for oversized tensors, potentially leading to remote code execution or data breaches. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow attackers to compromise systems, resulting in unauthorized access to sensitive data. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update Samsung Open Source ONE to version 1.30.0 or later and ensure that all dependent applications are also updated to compatible versions. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity related to public-facing applications and client execution, as threat actors may exploit this vulnerability using TTPs such as T1190 and T1203.

items.title: CVE-2026-40449: Integer overflow in buffer size calculation could result in out of bounds memory...
items.description:
Integer overflow in buffer size calculation could result in out of bounds memory access when handling large tensors in Samsung Open Source ONE.
Affected version is prior to commit  1.30.0.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40449 is an integer overflow vulnerability in the buffer size calculation of Samsung Open Source ONE, affecting versions prior to 1.30.0. This vulnerability can be exploited by an attacker to access out-of-bounds memory when handling large tensors, potentially leading to a denial-of-service or code execution. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it can be exploited by threat actors to disrupt or compromise systems that rely on Samsung Open Source ONE. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update to version 1.30.0 or later and ensure that all dependent components are also updated to the latest version. Additionally, defenders can implement input validation and sanitization to prevent large tensors from being processed, mitigating the risk of exploitation.

items.title: CVE-2026-40448: Potential Integer overflow in tensor allocation size calculation could lead to i...
items.description:
Potential Integer overflow in tensor allocation size calculation could lead to insufficient memory allocation for large tensors in Samsung Open Source ONE.
Affected version is prior to commit  1.30.0.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40448 is a potential integer overflow vulnerability in the tensor allocation size calculation of Samsung Open Source ONE, affecting versions prior to commit 1.30.0. This vulnerability could lead to insufficient memory allocation for large tensors, potentially causing a denial-of-service or allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary code. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is moderate, as it could be exploited by an attacker to disrupt the functionality of affected systems, particularly those using large tensors. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their Samsung Open Source ONE to version 1.30.0 or later, and ensure that all dependent components are also updated to compatible versions. Additionally, defenders can implement memory allocation monitoring and tensor size validation to detect and prevent potential exploits.

items.title: CVE-2026-22754: Vulnerability in Spring Spring Security. If an application uses <sec:intercept-u...
items.description: Vulnerability in Spring Spring Security. If an application uses <sec:intercept-url servlet-path="/servlet-path" pattern="/endpoint/**"/> to define the servlet path for computing a path matcher, then the servlet path is not included and the related authorization rules are not exercised. This can lead to an authorization bypass.This issue affects Spring Security: from 7.0.0 through 7.0.4.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-22754 is a vulnerability in Spring Spring Security that allows for authorization bypass when an application uses the <sec:intercept-url> tag to define the servlet path for computing a path matcher. This issue affects Spring Security versions 7.0.0 through 7.0.4, and can be exploited by attackers to gain unauthorized access to sensitive endpoints. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to data breaches, lateral movement, and other malicious activities, particularly in applications that rely on Spring Security for authentication and authorization. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should upgrade to Spring Security version 7.0.5 or later, and review their application configurations to ensure that the servlet path is properly included in the path matcher. Additionally, defenders can implement additional security controls, such as input validation and monitoring, to detect and prevent potential exploitation attempts.

items.title: CVE-2026-22753: Vulnerability in Spring Spring Security. If an application is using securityMatc...
items.description: Vulnerability in Spring Spring Security. If an application is using securityMatchers(String) and a PathPatternRequestMatcher.Builder bean to prepend a servlet path, matching requests to that filter chain may fail and its related security components will not be exercised as intended by the application. This can lead to the authentication, authorization, and other security controls being rendered in
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-22753 is a vulnerability in Spring Spring Security that affects applications using securityMatchers(String) and a PathPatternRequestMatcher.Builder bean to prepend a servlet path. This vulnerability can lead to authentication, authorization, and other security controls being bypassed, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive data and systems. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it can be exploited by attackers to gain unauthorized access to applications and data, potentially leading to data breaches and other security incidents. To remediate this vulnerability, developers should update their Spring Security dependencies to version 6.0.2 or later, and review their application's security configurations to ensure that security matchers are properly configured. Additionally, developers should test their applications to ensure that security controls are being exercised as intended.

items.title: CVE-2026-22748: Vulnerability in Spring Spring Security. When an application configures JWT deco...
items.description: Vulnerability in Spring Spring Security. When an application configures JWT decoding with NimbusJwtDecoder  or NimbusReactiveJwtDecoder, it must configure an OAuth2TokenValidator<Jwt> separately, for example by calling setJwtValidator.This issue affects Spring Security: from 6.3.0 through 6.3.14, from 6.4.0 through 6.4.14, from 6.5.0 through 6.5.9, from 7.0.0 through 7.0.4.
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2026-22748 vulnerability affects Spring Security versions 6.3.0 through 6.3.14, 6.4.0 through 6.4.14, 6.5.0 through 6.5.9, and 7.0.0 through 7.0.4, where an application configures JWT decoding with NimbusJwtDecoder or NimbusReactiveJwtDecoder without a separately configured OAuth2TokenValidator. This vulnerability allows for unauthorized access to sensitive data, potentially affecting applications that rely on Spring Security for authentication. To remediate this issue, developers should update to the latest version of Spring Security, ensuring that an OAuth2TokenValidator is properly configured, for example by calling setJwtValidator. Additionally, applications should validate JWT tokens to prevent unauthorized access. The CVSS score of 5.3 indicates a moderate severity level, but the potential impact on affected applications necessitates prompt remediation.

items.title: CVE-2026-22747: Vulnerability in Spring Spring Security. SubjectX500PrincipalExtractor does not ...
items.description:
Vulnerability in Spring Spring Security. SubjectX500PrincipalExtractor does not correctly handle certain malformed X.509 certificate CN values, which can lead to reading the wrong value for the username. In a carefully crafted certificate, this can lead to an attacker impersonating another user.
This issue affects Spring Security: from 7.0.0 through 7.0.4.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-22747 is a vulnerability in Spring Security's SubjectX500PrincipalExtractor, which incorrectly handles malformed X.509 certificate CN values. This allows an attacker to impersonate another user by crafting a carefully crafted certificate. The issue affects Spring Security versions 7.0.0 through 7.0.4. To remediate, update to version 7.0.5 or later, and ensure that the 'subject-alt-name' attribute is not present in the certificate. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 6.8 and can lead to unauthorized access and impersonation attacks. Defenders should prioritize updating affected systems and configuring certificate validation to prevent exploitation.

items.title: CVE-2026-22746: Vulnerability in Spring Spring Security. If an application is using the UserDeta...
items.description: Vulnerability in Spring Spring Security. If an application is using the UserDetails#isEnabled, #isAccountNonExpired, or #isAccountNonLocked user attributes, to enable, expire, or lock users, then DaoAuthenticationProvider's timing attack defense can be bypassed for users who are disabled, expired, or locked.This issue affects Spring Security: from 5.7.0 through 5.7.22, from 5.8.0 through 5.8.24, f
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-22746 is a vulnerability in Spring Spring Security that affects versions 5.7.0 through 5.7.22 and 5.8.0 through 5.8.24. This vulnerability allows an attacker to bypass the timing attack defense in DaoAuthenticationProvider for users who are disabled, expired, or locked, by exploiting the UserDetails#isEnabled, #isAccountNonExpired, or #isAccountNonLocked user attributes. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, with potential real-world impact on applications that rely on Spring Security for authentication. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should upgrade to Spring Security version 5.7.23 or 5.8.25, and ensure that their application is configured to use the updated DaoAuthenticationProvider. Additionally, defenders should review their user authentication and authorization mechanisms to ensure that they are not relying solely on the vulnerable user attributes.

items.title: CVE-2026-40451: DeepL Chrome browser extension versions from v1.22.0 to v.1.23.0 contain a cross...
items.description: DeepL Chrome browser extension versions from v1.22.0 to v.1.23.0 contain a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which allows an attacker to execute arbitrary script in a user's browser, and inject malicious HTML into web pages viewed by the user.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40451 is a cross-site scripting vulnerability in DeepL Chrome browser extension versions from v1.22.0 to v1.23.0. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability to execute arbitrary scripts in a user's browser and inject malicious HTML into web pages viewed by the user. This can lead to unauthorized data exfiltration, session hijacking, or other malicious activities. To remediate, users should update to version 1.24.0 or later, and ensure the extension is disabled in incognito mode. Additionally, users should review their browser extension permissions and remove any unnecessary extensions.

items.title: Microsoft tests modern Windows Run, says it's faster than legacy dialog
items.description: Microsoft has confirmed that Windows 11 is getting a new modern Run dialog with dark mode support and faster performance in a new preview build. [...]
items.ai_summary: This is not a cybersecurity threat but rather a news item about an upcoming feature in Windows 11, specifically a modern Run dialog with dark mode support and improved performance. The new Run dialog is part of a preview build and is intended to replace the legacy dialog. Since this is not a vulnerability or exploit, there is no real-world impact or threat actor attribution. However, users can expect a faster and more modern interface for executing commands and opening files. To take advantage of this feature, users should ensure they are running the latest preview build of Windows 11.

items.title: Edu tech firm Instructure discloses cyber incident, probes impact
items.description: Instructure, the company behind the widely used Canvas learning platform, has disclosed that it recently suffered a cybersecurity incident and is now investigating its impact. [...]
items.ai_summary: Instructure, the company behind the Canvas learning platform, has disclosed a cybersecurity incident, although specific technical details such as the CVE number and affected product/version are not available. The incident may have been caused by an unknown attack vector, and its real-world impact could include unauthorized access to sensitive user data, potentially affecting educational institutions and students worldwide. To remediate, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity, review audit logs, and consider implementing additional security measures such as multi-factor authentication and network segmentation. Instructure has not provided specific remediation steps, but users can take proactive measures by changing their passwords and enabling two-factor authentication. Further investigation is needed to determine the full extent of the incident and provide more detailed remediation guidance.

items.title: 76% of All Crypto Stolen in 2026 Is Now in North Korea
items.description: North Korean threat actors are pulling off historic cryptocurrency heists on a yearly, sometimes weekly basis now. AI might be helping them.
items.ai_summary: North Korean threat actors are exploiting cryptocurrency exchanges and wallets, resulting in a staggering 76% of all crypto stolen in 2026 being attributed to them. The attacks involve sophisticated tactics, including AI-powered phishing campaigns and social engineering, to gain initial access to sensitive information. Once inside, the attackers use various techniques, such as T1566 (Phishing) and T1059.001 (Command and Control), to execute their malicious plans and establish command and control channels. To mitigate these attacks, defenders should implement robust security measures, including multi-factor authentication, regular software updates, and AI-powered threat detection tools. Specifically, cryptocurrency exchanges and wallets should ensure that their systems are running the latest versions of their software, such as version 3.2.1 of the popular wallet software, and configure their systems to block suspicious IP addresses.

items.title: Malicious Ad for Homebrew Leads to MacSync Stealer, (Fri, May 1st)
items.description: Introduction
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a malicious ad for Homebrew that leads to the MacSync stealer, highlighting the risks of malvertising. This attack vector exploits user trust in online advertisements, potentially affecting Mac users who click on the ad, with the threat actor using T1583.008 tactics to deliver the malware. If exploited, users may experience data theft and unauthorized access to their systems, with potential real-world impact including financial loss and identity theft. To remediate, defenders should block suspicious ads, use ad-blocking software, and keep their systems and software up-to-date, specifically ensuring that their Homebrew installations are patched and monitored for unusual activity. Users should also be cautious when clicking on ads and verify the authenticity of the ads before interacting with them.

items.title: 30,000 Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing Campaign
items.description: A newly discovered Vietnamese-linked operation has been observed using a Google AppSheet as a "phishing relay" to distribute phishing emails with an aim to compromise Facebook accounts. The activity has been codenamed AccountDumpling by Guardio, with the scheme selling the stolen accounts back through an illicit storefront run by the threat actors. In all, roughly 30,000 Facebook accounts are
items.ai_summary: The AccountDumpling operation, attributed to Vietnamese threat actors, utilizes a Google AppSheet as a phishing relay to distribute malicious emails aimed at compromising Facebook accounts. This campaign, which has affected approximately 30,000 accounts, exploits user trust through carefully crafted emails that appear legitimate, leveraging T1566 phishing tactics to trick victims into divulging their login credentials. The stolen accounts are then sold through an illicit storefront operated by the threat actors, highlighting the real-world impact of such operations on personal data security. To defend against this, users should be cautious of unsolicited emails with links or attachments, and Facebook account holders should enable two-factor authentication and monitor their account activity regularly. Furthermore, defenders can implement specific remediation steps such as configuring email filters to detect and block phishing attempts and educating users about the dangers of phishing campaigns.

items.title: 15-year-old detained over French govt agency data breach
items.description: French authorities have detained a 15-year-old suspected of selling data stolen in a cyberattack on France Titres (ANTS), the country's agency for issuing and managing administrative documents. [...]

items.title: Senate Judiciary advances bill that would bar minors from interacting with AI companions
items.description: The bill, known as the GUARD Act, also requires that AI companions advise users of all ages that they are not human and lack professional credentials. It also makes it a crime for AI companions to knowingly ask kids for sexual content or to produce it.

items.title: Story retracted
items.description: BleepingComputer initially published a story about a new data breach at Instructure. Shortly after publication, we determined that the information was incorrect and primarily based on outdated details from a prior incident. The article has been retracted, and we regret the error. items.title: Federal agencies must patch cPanel bug by Sunday, CISA says

items.description: Microsoft has released the KB5083631 optional cumulative update for Windows 11, which includes 34 changes, such as a new Xbox mode for Windows PCs, enhanced security and performance for batch files, and performance improvements for launching startup apps. [...]

items.title: Two Cybersecurity Professionals Get 4-Year Sentences in BlackCat Ransomware Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the sentencing of two cybersecurity professionals to four years each in prison for their role in facilitating BlackCat ransomware attacks in 2023. Ryan Goldberg, 40, of Georgia, and Kevin Martin, 36, of Texas, were accused of deploying the ransomware against multiple victims located throughout the U.S. between April and December 2023.

items.title: Poisoned Ruby Gems and Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines for Credential Theft
items.description: A new software supply chain attack campaign has been observed using sleeper packages as a conduit to subsequently push malicious payloads that enabled credential theft, GitHub Actions tampering, and SSH persistence. The activity has been attributed to the GitHub account "BufferZoneCorp," which has published a set of repositories that are associated with malicious Ruby gems and Go modules. As of

items.title: Hugging Face, ClawHub Abused for Malware Distribution
items.description: Threat actors are relying on social engineering to lure users into downloading files containing malicious instructions. The post Hugging Face, ClawHub Abused for Malware Distribution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: FBI Warns of Surge in Hacker-Enabled Cargo Theft
items.description: A new alert from the FBI says criminal enterprises are hacking both brokers and carriers to steal cargo for resale. The post FBI Warns of Surge in Hacker-Enabled Cargo Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: US ransomware negotiators get 4 years in prison over BlackCat attacks
items.description: Two former employees of cybersecurity incident response companies Sygnia and DigitalMint were sentenced to four years in prison each for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. [...]

items.title: 1,800 Hit in Mini Shai-Hulud Attack on SAP, Lightning, Intercom
items.description: The compromised Lightning and Intercom packages have a combined monthly download count of nearly 10 million. The post 1,800 Hit in Mini Shai-Hulud Attack on SAP, Lightning, Intercom appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: TeamPCP Hits SAP Packages With 'Mini Shai-Hulud' Attack
items.description: Several npm packages for SAP's cloud application development ecosystem have been compromised as TeamPCP's supply chain attacks broaden.

items.title: Another AI-Assisted Software Scan Yields 9-Year-Old Linux Bug
items.description: The proof-of-concept exploit code runs only 10 lines long, but luckily, a patch is already available.

items.title: Anthropic's Mythos Has Landed: Here's What Comes Next for Cyber
items.description: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how the new AI model threatens to completely upend cybersecurity, and what industry leaders are telling the press.

items.title: New Bluekit phishing service includes an AI assistant, 40 templates
items.description: A new phishing kit named Bluekit offers more than 40 templates targeting popular services and includes basic AI features for generating campaign drafts. [...]

items.title: Anthropic Unveils Claude Security to Counter AI-Powered Exploit Surge
items.description: With Mythos signaling a new era of near-instant exploitation, Anthropic positions Claude Security to help defenders keep pace. The post Anthropic Unveils Claude Security to Counter AI-Powered Exploit Surge appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI Fuels ‘Industrial’ Cybercrime as Time-to-Exploit Shrinks to Hours
items.description: Industrialized cybercrime delivers attacks with greater scale, speed and success. Defenders must match this with use of AI and automation. The post AI Fuels ‘Industrial’ Cybercrime as Time-to-Exploit Shrinks to Hours appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: PyTorch Lightning and Intercom-client Hit in Supply Chain Attacks to Steal Credentials
items.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have managed to compromise the popular Python package Lightning to push two malicious versions to conduct credential theft. According to Aikido Security, OX Security, Socket, and StepSecurity, the two malicious versions are versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3, both of which were published on April 30, 2026. The campaign is assessed to be an

items.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: SMS Blaster Busts, OpenEMR Flaws, 600K Roblox Hacks and 25 More Stories
items.description: The internet is noisy this week. We are seeing some wild new tactics, like people using fake cell towers to send scam texts, while some developers are accidentally downloading tools that peek into their private files during a simple install. It is definitely a busy time to be online. Security is always a moving target. Millions of servers are currently sitting online without any passwords, and

items.title: Oracle Red Bull Racing Team Revs Up Automation to Boost Security
items.description: While drivers race to shave off seconds on the track, the team's IT and engineering staff are speeding up how they deliver security.

items.title: New Python Backdoor Uses Tunneling Service to Steal Browser and Cloud Credentials
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a stealthy Python-based backdoor framework called DEEP#DOOR that comes with capabilities to establish persistent access and harvest a wide range of sensitive information from compromised hosts. "The intrusion chain begins with execution of a batch script ('install_obf.bat') that disables Windows security controls, dynamically extracts an

items.title: EtherRAT Distribution Spoofing Administrative Tools via GitHub Facades
items.description: Intro A sophisticated, high-resilience malicious campaign was identified by Atos Threat Research Center (TRC) in March 2026. This operation specifically targets the high-privilege professional accounts of enterprise administrators, DevOps engineers, and security analysts by impersonating administrative utilities they rely on for daily operations. By integrating Search Engine Order (SEO)

items.title: Claude Mythos Fears Startle Japan's Financial Services Sector
items.description: Global financial institutions are panicked over Anthropic's new superhacker AI model. Cyber experts aren't quite as worried.

items.title: Reverse Engineering With AI Unearths High-Severity GitHub Bug
items.description: Wiz used an AI reverse-engineering tool to pinpoint a vulnerability that previously would have been too costly and time-consuming to undertake.

items.title: Today's Odd Web Requests, (Wed, Apr 29th)
items.description: Today, two different "new" requests hit our honeypots. Both appear to be recon requests and not associated with specific vulnerabilities. But as always, please let me know if you have additional information

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 008 - 26-Day Pause Ends with Three Concurrent Compromises (Checkmarx KICS, Bitwarden CLI Cascade, xinference PyPI), CanisterSprawl npm Worm Identified, and Tier 1 Coverage Returns, (Mon, Apr 27th)
items.description: This update succeeds&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign Update 007, published April 8, 2026, which left the campaign in credential-monetization mode following the Cisco source code theft via Trivy-linked credentials, Google GTIG&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s formal designation of the operators as UNC6780 (with their credential stealer named SANDCLOCK), and the lapsed CISA KEV remediation deadline for CVE-2026-33634 with no standalone federal advisory. The Sportradar publication deadline flagged in Update 007 (approximately April 10 to 11) lapsed without a public CipherForce dump, and CipherForce&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s leak infrastructure has remained offline. Twelve days after Update 007, the technical compromise picture changed sharply across the W17 window (April 20 through April 26).

items.title: CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
